description_long: This is a two-story house with a distinctive Old West or frontier architectural style, featuring a wraparound wooden porch supported by slender columns. The exterior walls are finished in a light beige color, framed by prominent wooden trim in a warm brown hue. The roof is a low-pitched hip style, also clad in brown wood, and extends outward to form a protective overhang above the porch. Multiple rectangular windows with blue-tinted glass are symmetrically arranged on both floors, providing ample natural light. The main entrance is centrally located beneath the porch, flanked by windows and accessible via the wooden deck. The overall design evokes a rustic, historical ambiance typical of 19th-century American towns.
description: A two-story, Old West-style house with a wraparound wooden porch, brown trim, and symmetrically arranged blue windows. The hip roof and rustic woodwork give it a historical frontier appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, wraparound porch, and symmetrical window arrangement.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the porch and the two-story window placement.
description_view_2: Rear view with plain beige walls and no visible windows or doors.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the porch extension and window alignment.
attribute_entryway_design: Central wooden door beneath a wraparound porch with columns and a wide overhanging roof
